facade_colour: warm red brick with buff/cream decorative accents; partially obscured by large tree

hcd_data.architectural_style: House-form, Detached, Bay-and-Gable (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. The property retains original building features including its cross-gabled roofline, double-height bay window, and brick cladding. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. The property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red brick with buff/cream decorative accents; partially obscured by large tree
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Building partially obscured by large mature tree; aging brick in fair condition
photo_observations.window_details: Windows largely obscured by tree; bay window area visible at ground and first floor per HCD
photo_observations.door_details: Entrance obscured by tree; beside bay window
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Double-height projecting bay spanning ground and first floors, Bay-and-Gable typology
photo_observations.decorative_details: Polychromatic brickwork with buff accents visible through branches; cross-gable form
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Metal balcony railing added at second floor; some replacement windows possible
photo_observations.notes: Primary photo partially obstructed by large tree. Bay window visible on ground floor. Victorian bay-and-gable form clearly legible. Decorative diamond tile work visible at entrance level (similar to 116 Bellevue).

notes: Photo heavily obscured by large mature tree. Bay-and-Gable form discernible but details hard to confirm.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, detached, bay-and-gable typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, bay window, cornice.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: The property retains original building features including its cross-gabled roofline, double-height bay window, and brick cladding. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.

facade_description: Row two-storey bay-and-gable in warm red brick with buff/cream decorative accents; partially obscured by large tree under a cross-gable roof. Features include a double-height three-sided projecting bay, decorative bargeboards and polychromatic brickwork. Metal balcony railing added at second floor; some replacement windows possible. Pre-1889 construction.
heritage_summary: The property retains original building features including its cross-gabled roofline, double-height bay window, and brick cladding. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.
